original: St Martin I ends his reign as Catholic Pope
Ali ibn Abu Talib elected the 4th Caliph of the Rashidun Caliphate
Pope Adeodatus II's (Deusdetit II) reign ends with his death
Floris II de Vette becomes Count of Holland
Charles the Good becomes Count of Flanders
Union of Kalmar established between Denmark, Sweden and Norway
Pánfilo de Narváez departs Spain to explore Florida with five ships and 600 men; by 1536, only four men are still alive
Matsunaga Hisahide assassinates the 13th Ashikaga shōgun, Ashikaga Yoshiteru
Anti-English uprising in Ireland
Battle at Hardenberg: Spanish troops beat rebels
Brabant: Duke of Parma beats French mercenaries
Netherlands, England and France sign 12 year Covenant
Battle of Montes Claros: English and Portuguese armies decisively defeat Spain, securing Portugal's independence from Spain in the last battle of the Portuguese Restoration War
Massachusetts orders priest to leave the colony
French troops occupy Philipsburg at Rhine
American colonials capture Louisburg, Cape Breton Island from French
Design of Madonnina statue chosen, symbol of the city of Milan that stands atop spire of the Cathedral, designed by sculptor Giuseppe Perego
Cúcuta, Colombia is founded by Juana Rangel de Cuéllar
Battle of Bunker Hill (actually it was Breed's Hill)
French Revolution: During the meeting of the Estates-General, the Third Estate proclaims itself the 'National Assembly'
A 1471 printed copy of Boccaccio's Decameron sells in England for £2,260 - then the highest price for a book, during the infamous sale of the library of the Duke of Roxburghe
Cannon duel fought between the British fort of Rusteburg and the kraton (palace) of Yogyakarta on the island of Java
American frigate USS Guerriere captained by Stephen Decatur captures Algerian frigate Mashouda, killing its famous captain corsair Raïs Hamidou during US-Algerian War
US Bureau of Indian Affairs established

King of Hawaii Kamehameha III issues Edict of toleration which gives Roman Catholics freedom to worship in the Hawaiian Islands. The Hawaii Catholic Church and the Cathedral of Our Lady of Peace is later established
Paddle-wheeler "G P Griffith" burns off Mentor, Ohio, killing 206 people
Heavy French and British bombing of Sebastopol, Crimea: 2,000+ killed
Republican Party opens its first national convention as party members meet in Philadelphia
Battle of Boonville, MI-Brigadier General Lyon defeats Confederate forces
Battle at Middleburg, Virginia - 1st Rhode Island Cavalry routed trying to take the town
Battle of Aldie, Confederates fail to drive back Union in Virginia
Naval Engagement at Warsaw Sound GA-USS Weehawken vs CSS Atlanta
Travelers Insurance Co of Hartford chartered (1st accident insurer)
-18] Confederate troops pull back out Solves/lost Mt, Georgia
640m long ponton bridge over James River, Virginia finished
Confederate General John Bell Hood replaces General Johnston as head of the Army of Tennessee
Skirmish at Mud Creek/Noyes's (Nose) Creek, Georgia
1st to hit 2 HRs; & score 5 runs in 9 inn NL game (George Hall, A's)
Battle of Rosebud/Battle Where Girl Saved Her Brother
Providence Grays MLB pitcher John M. Ward throws a perfect game beating the Buffalo Bisons, 5-0; second perfect game in National League in 6 days; next one takes 84 years
Tornado kills 130 in Iowa
US Ship Canal (W 225th St) in the Bronx is completed, cutting Marble Hill off from Manhattan
US State Department appoints New Jersey educator William Frank Powell as Envoy to Haiti, and Chargé d’Affaires for Dominican Republic
The United States Navy Hospital Corps is established
The College Board introduces its first standardized test, the forerunner to the SAT
US Congress pass the New Lands Reclamation Act, which establishes a fund from sale of public lands to build irrigation dams for arid Western lands
Belgium government of De Broqueville forms
MLB Chicago Cubs George "Zip" Zabel relieves with 2 outs in 1st and winds up with 4-3 19-inning win over Brooklyn Dodgers at home, in longest relief job ever
1st national congress of Sarekat Islam at Bandoeng Java
US troops under General Pershing march into Mexico
"Barney Google" cartoon strip, by Billy De Beck, premieres
Dutch 2nd Chamber accept Anti-revolution law
First flight across the South Atlantic Ocean arrives in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, piloted by Gago Coutinho and Sacadura Cabral. First use of the sextant in air navigation.
Bonus Army: around a thousand World War I veterans amass at the United States Capitol as the US Senate considers a bill that would give them certain benefits
Oil tanker Cymbeline explodes in Montreal, Canada
Kansas City Massacre: 1 FBI agent, 4 cops & 1 gangster killed by mob
Japan declares war on China
Last public guillotining in France. Eugen Weidmann, a convicted murderer, is guillotined in Versailles outside the prison Saint-Pierre
France asks Germany for terms of surrender in World War II
General De Gaulle departs Bordeaux for London
Germany occupiers ration bread in Holland
USSR occupies Estonia
World War II: sinking of the RMS Lancastria by the Luftwaffe near Saint-Nazaire, France
-19] French troops under Lattre de Tssigny conquer Elba
Iceland dissolves its union with Denmark and declares itself a Republic
Resistance fighter and poet Col Blake arrives in London
Day of Unity in West Germany (National Day)
SW Bell inaugurates mobile telephone commercial service, St Louis
Earnest Reuter becomes mayor of Berlin
Pan Am Airways' first round-the-world civil air service departs from New York City
1st kidney transplant (Chicago)
Egypt, Lebanon, Saudi Arabia & Syria sign security pact
2 mine cave-ins at Charleroi, Belgium
Most runs scored in 1 inning (17 by Red Sox)
Riots in East Germany for reunification
Televised Senate Army McCarthy hearings ends
"So Rare" by Jimmy Dorsey Orchestra peaks at #2
Tuskegee boycott begins (Blacks boycotted city stores)
The Wooden Roller Coaster opens at Playland in the Pacific National Exhibition in Vancouver, Canada, and remains open to this day
FIFA World Cup Final, Estadio Nacional, Santiago, Chile: Brazil go 1-down early but recover to beat Czechoslovakia, 3-1
British House of Commons debates Profumo-Christine Keeler affair
Supreme Court rules against Bible reading/prayer in public schools
11.08" (28.14 cm) of rainfall, Holly, Colorado (state 24-hour record)
British rock band The Kinks arrive in NYC beginning their 1st US tour
First bombing run by a B-52 occurs 50 km north of Saigon
Peter Green, British blues-rock guitartist joins John Mayall's Bluebreakers
"Somebody To Love" by Jefferson Airplane peaks at #5
China becomes the world's fourth thermonuclear power by detonating a hydrogen bomb
Longest doubleheader 9:15 (Tigers & Athletics)
Belgium government of Eyskens-Merlot forms
KQEC TV channel 32 in San Francisco, California (PBS) begins broadcasting
Ohio Express' "Yummy Yummy Yummy" goes gold
British rock band Led Zeppelin begin their last European tour
"Long Haired Lover From Liverpool" single by Little Jimmy Osmond peaks at #38
9 firefighters are killed in the Hotel Vendome fire in Boston
Five men arrested after trying to bug Democratic National Committee office in Watergate Complex, Washington
The Provisional Irish Republican Army bombs the Houses of Parliament in London, injuring 11 people and causing extensive damage
Voters in Northern Mariana Islands approve commonwealth status with US
ABA (Nets, Pacers, Nuggets & Spurs) merges into NBA
Indonesia annexes Portuguese East-Timor
Soweto Uprising: Government deploys armed military to quell student protests; hundreds of civilians and children killed
The Provisional Irish Republican Army kill an RUC officer and kidnap another near Crossmaglen, County Armagh
Battle between Muslims & Christians in Cairo, 14 killed
18th NASA Space Shuttle Mission (51-G): Discovery 5 launches
US Chief Justice Warren Earl Burger resigns and Antonin Scalia nominated as his replacement
With the death of the last individual, the Dusky Seaside Sparrow becomes extinct
Microsoft releases MS DOS 4.0
Soyuz TM-5 launches

Stella Nickell is sentenced to 90 years in prison in the first product tampering murder case after she poisoned Excedrin capsules with cyanide to kill her husband, Bruce Nickell, and a stranger, Sue Snow
US beats Guatemala 2-1 in 3rd round of 1990 world soccer cup
Country entertainer Minnie Pearl suffers a stroke at 78
South Africa abolishes last of its apartheid laws
Connecticut Governor Lowell Weicker and WFAN DJDon Imuschange places for 1 day
Slaughtering by Inkhata-followers at Boipatong, South Africa, kills 42
Indians' Carlos Baerga hits 3 home runs against Detroit
Howard Stern Radio Show premieres in Syracuse, New York on WAQX 95.7 FM
The Fifteenth Amendment to the Irish Constitution, which removes the prohibition on divorce, is signed into law following a vote the previous year
NHL announces it will add Nashville in 1998, Atlanta in 1999 & Minneapolis-St Paul & Columbus, Ohio in 2000
Wynton Marsalis releases his "Blood on the Fields" album (first jazz work to win the Pulitzer Prize for Music - 1997)
First day of legal same-sex marriage in California
France's Socialist Party wins a majority in the legislative election
Greek voters return to the polls after the failed May 6 election
9 people are shot and killed inside Emanuel AME Church in Charleston, South Carolina, by a 21-year-old gunman
Collision at sea between U.S.S. Fitzgerald and ACX Crystal, a Philippine cargo ship kills 7 US sailors in Japanese waters
Forest fires in Pedrógão Grande, Portugal begin, kill 62 people, more than 1,600 firefighters fight 156 fires
"The Incredibles 2" sets a box office record for an animated release, earning $180 million its opening weekend
Historic deal signed between Greece and Macedonia to end dispute over Macedonia's name - changing to North Macedonia
Ivan Duque is elected President of Columbia
Play "Fairview" by Jackie Sibblies Drury premieres off-Broadway at the  Soho Repertory Theatre (Pulitzer Prize for Drama 2019)
World electric speed record broken by Jaguar Vector V20E in battery-powered boat at 88.61 mph (142.60 kph) Coniston Water, English Lake District
Mayor of Phoenix, Arizona, Kate Gallego apologies for local police threatening to shoot African American family after their four-year shoplifted a doll
Michael R. Jackson's musical "Ä Strange Loop" opens off-Broadway in New York (Pulitzer Prize for Drama 2020)
American actor Danny Masterson charged with raping three women. after a 3-year investigation
First major breakthrough in treating COVID-19 using steroid dexamethasone announced by Oxford University
Former police officer who shot Rayshard Brooks in Atlanta charged with his murder and aggravated assault
China launches its Shenzhou-12 spacecraft with three astronauts arriving at its new space station Tiangong six and a half hours later

"Running Up That Hill" single by Kate Bush goes to #1 on the UK chart; originally released in 1985, the song  was featured in sci-fi television show "Stranger Things", its record 44 year climb to the top also makes Bush (63) the oldest female artist to score a No.1
Philippines accuses Chinese coastguard of “a brazen act of aggression” after a confrontation in the contested Spratly Islands escalating the tension in the area
At least 59 Palestinians in Gaza die after Israeli tanks fire into a crowd waiting for humanitarian aid
Stanley Cup Final, Amerant Bank Arena, Sunrise, FL: Florida Panthers win their second consecutive title, once again defeating the Edmonton Oilers, 5-1,  in Game 6; MVP: Sam Bennett (Panthers C)
Vlad IIIthe Impaler (Dracula) attempts to assassinate OttomanSultan Mehmed IIin a night attack at Târgoviște, its failure forces him to retreat from Wallachia
Battle of Deptford Bridge - forces under KingHenry VIIsoundly defeat Cornish rebels led by Michael An Gof
Trial of English Catholic Cardinal John Fisher for treason at Westminster Hall for declaring KingHenry VIIIis not the Supreme Head of the Church in England. He is found guilty and later executed
Future explorerJames Cookjoins the Royal Navy as an able-bodied seaman at Wapping, East London
After a lengthy stay in Europe, future US PresidentJohn Adamsand his wifeAbigail Adamsreturn to Boston
Charles Goodyearobtains his first rubber patent
Indian Wars: Battle of White Bird Canyon - theNez Percedefeat the US Cavalry at White Bird Canyon in the Idaho Territory
Polar explorersFridtjof Nansenand Frederick Jackson meet by chance in Franz Josef Land, the Arctic
The League to Enforce Peace is organised at Independence Hall in Philadelphia withWilliam Howard Taftas president; its program anticipates the League of Nations
Amelia Earhartleaves Newfoundland to become 1st woman (passenger) to fly Atlantic (as a passenger in a plane piloted by Wilmer Stultz)
Player-managerJoe Croninof Red Sox hits two 3-run pinch home runs
Hitlersecretly meets with Field Marshals von Rundstedt andRommelin Marjival, Soissons, France to assess response to Normandy Invasion
US Supreme Court Justice William O. Douglas stays executions of spies Julius &Ethel Rosenbergscheduled for next day their 14th anniversary
CIA exile army lands in Guatemala. Organised byJohn Foster Dullesand United Fruit Co.
Rocky MarcianobeatsEzzard Charlesby unanimous points decision in his 3rd world heavyweight boxing title defense at Yankee Stadium, NYC
Golda Meirbegins her term as Israel's foreign minister
"Things Fall Apart" by Nigerian writerChinua Achebepublished by Heinemann - considered most widely read book in African literature
Radio Moscow reports execution of Hungarian ex-premierImre Nagy
Eamon de Valera elected President of Ireland
Ted Williamshit his 500th HR
Chicago CubLou Brockis 2nd to HR into Polo Grounds right-center field bleachers
US Open Men's Golf, Oakmont CC:Jack Nicklauswins his first major title by 3 strokes in an 18-hole playoff withArnold Palmer
Barbra Streisandperforms a "A Happening in Central Park" concert in NYC
Carole King's album "Tapestry" goes to #1 on US album charts and stays there for 15 weeks
Chilean PresidentSalvador Allendeforms a new socialist government
Dolly Partonrecords her song "I Will Always Love You" (later a huge hit forWhitney Houston) for RCA in Nashville
"Cheeseburger In Paradise" byJimmy Buffettpeaks at #32
Ron Guidrysets Yankee record with 18 strike-outs
Columbia Records releasesBruce Springsteen's fifth studio album "The River"; the 2-record set becomes a global smash, going top five in 8 countries
President of ArgentinaLeopoldo Galtieriresigns as commander in chief of the army and as president after leading Argentina to a disastrous defeat against the British in the Falkland Islands War
USPresident Reagan's 1st UN General Assembly address "We must serve mankind through genuine disarmament."
Ed WarrenandLorraine Warrenexorcise a "werewolf demon" from Bill Ramsey, although lack of photo or video evidence has called this claim into question
John TurnersucceedsPierre Trudeauas Prime Minister of Canada
Givens' Family reportsMike Tysonbeats his wifeRobin Givens
The body of the 12th US President,Zachary Taylor, is exhumed to test how he died; rumors had persisted since his death in 1850 of arsenic poisoning - no evidence of this was found
Philadelphia 76ers tradeCharles Barkleyto Phoenix Suns
O.J. Simpsondoes not turn himself in on murder charges, forcing LA police to chase his Ford Bronco for 1½ hours before he eventually gives up (seen live on national TV)
"Moneyball", a book about the 2003 Oakland Athletics baseball team and GM Billy Beane's sabermetric approach, inspired byBill James, is published
Sci-Fi authorAnne McCaffreyinducted into the Science Fiction Hall of Fame
Dance hit "Wake Me Up" released by Swedish DJ and record producerAviciiwith vocals by Aloe Blacc
US Treasury announces that the image ofAlexander Hamiltonwill be replaced on the US $10 bill by an image of a woman
Bill Cosbytrial for aggravated indecent assault declared a mistrial after jurors couldn't reach a verdict
ActivistJoshua Wongcalls on Hong Kong leader Carrie Lam to resign after leaving prison amid street protests the day before of over 2 million people.
Former Egyptian PresidentMohamed Morsidies after collapsing during a court appearance in Cairo
MLB Los Angeles Angels designated hitterShohei Ohtanihits 150th career home run in a 10-9 loss to Royals at Kauffman Stadium in Kansas City, Missouri
English navigatorFrancis Drakelands on the coast of California at what becomes known as Drakes Bay, for ship repairs; claims area on behalf of Queen Elizabeth, and names it "Nova Albion"
Mumtaz Mahal dies during childbirth. Her husband, Mughal emperor Shah Jahan I, then spends more than 20 years building her tomb, the Taj Mahal
Statue of Liberty arrives in NYC aboard French ship `Isere'
First US poliomyelitis epidemic breaks out in Rutland, Vermont
Peter Schickele, Robert Dennis and Stanley Walden's musical revue "Oh! Calcutta!" opens off-Broadway at the Eden Theatre, NYC; noted for it being performed almost entirely in the nude
US PresidentJoe Bidensigns into law the Juneteenth National Independence Day Act making June 19th a federal holiday commemorating emancipation
Joseph of Cupertino, Italian saint, born in Copertino, Apulia, Kingdom of Naples (d. 1663)
